verdict: 2026-06-26 10:41:02 PDT — The deployment fix itself is sound: `exec node server/src/index.js` matches the reproduced PID-1 failure mode, fits the repo's existing direct Node server entrypoint, and avoids adding an init/process-manager dependency without a concrete win for this app. The test design needs tightening before implementation: it currently misses an existing Railway config test and uses weaker string-shape assertions than the actual invariant.

findings.title: Scope misses the existing Railway config test
findings.claim: The sketch adds `server/test/railway-startcommand.test.js` and AC6 excludes `server/test/health.test.js`, but `health.test.js` already asserts `cfg.deploy.startCommand === 'npm start'`. Changing `railway.json` as proposed will make `npm test` fail, so AC7 cannot pass within the proposed scope. Adding a second static Railway config test also duplicates the repo's existing config-contract test.
findings.alternative: Update the existing Railway config test in `server/test/health.test.js` to assert the new start command while keeping the build and healthcheck assertions, and include that file in the allowed scope. Only add a new test file if it covers a separate contract.
findings.win: Makes the gate pass, keeps one canonical Railway config contract, and removes duplicate JSON-read/assertion code.

findings.title: Regression guard is weaker than the PID-1 contract
findings.claim: The proposed guard checks that the command starts with `exec`, includes `node`, and excludes `npm`, but those fragments do not prove Node is the direct PID-1 process. Wrapper forms can satisfy those checks while reintroducing signal-delivery risk. The actual accepted invariant is the known-good command `exec node server/src/index.js`.
findings.alternative: Assert exact equality to `exec node server/src/index.js` in the Railway config test, or tokenize the command and assert exactly `exec`, `node`, `server/src/index.js` with no extra wrapper tokens.
findings.win: Replaces partial string heuristics with one declarative invariant and eliminates false positives for commands that no longer match the proven production-safe shape.
